classObjective: Determine whether a function represented by a graph, equation, table, or real-world situation is discrete, continuous, or neither.
workedExamples: 3-3, Example 1 — Classify Functions from Graphs as Discrete or Continuous
3-3, Example 2 — Classify Real-World Functions from Graphs as Discrete or Continuous
warmUp: Give an example of a discrete situation and a continuous situation from daily life
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: discrete vs. continuous context; counting vs. measuring quantities; graph appearance differences
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice classifying functions as discrete, continuous, or neither from multiple representations
independentPractice: Practice problems classifying real-world and mathematical functions by their continuity type
assessment: Exit ticket: classify whether 'number of books on a shelf' is discrete or continuous and explain why
capReflection: How does knowing whether a function is discrete or continuous help you choose the right representation?
